name: Cueva Ventana, Ermita de Nuestra Señora de la Esperanza, Iglesia de San Martín – circular route
name_original: Cueva Ventana, Ermita de Nuestra Señora de la Esperanza, Iglesia de San Martín – ruta circular
region_intro: Hiking around Torrecilla En Cameros offers diverse terrain within the Sierra de Cameros mountains, bisected by the Iregua River. The region features dramatic mountainous settings, picturesque valleys, and gorges, providing a mix of challenging ascents and gentler riverside paths. Notable natural features include the Sierra de Cebollera Natural Park with its beech and pine forests, as well as canyons carved by rivers like the Leza. This landscape provides varied hiking trails around Torrecilla En Cameros for different preferences. Best hiking trails around Torrecilla En Cameros The most popular hiking route is Cueva Ventana, Ermita de Nuestra Señora de la Esperanza, Iglesia de San Martín – circular route, a 4.1 miles (6.6 km) trail that takes 2 hours to complete. This moderate route offers varied scenery including caves and historic chapels. Another top favourite among local hikers is Cemetery – Viguera Waterfall (Chorrón de Viguera) loop from Viguera, a moderate 5.2 miles (8.4 km) path. This trail leads to the Chorrón de Viguera waterfall, showcasing the region's water features. Local hikers also love the Viewpoint – San Pedro Apostol Church loop from Viguera, a 6.2 miles (10.0 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 3 hours 20 minutes. Hiking around Torrecilla En Cameros is defined by mountainous terrain, river valleys, and dense forests within the Sierra de Cameros. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy riverside walks to more challenging ascents. The routes in Torrecilla En Cameros are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from more than 30 reviews. More than 300 hikers have used komoot to explore Torrecilla En Cameros's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Torrecilla En Cameros?
A: There are over 50 hiking trails around Torrecilla En Cameros, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels and preferences. These include 8 easy routes, 28 moderate routes, and 14 difficult routes.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for families or beginners?
A: Yes, there are several easy hiking trails perfect for families or beginners. One such route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40011207" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Olmo de El Rasillo – Ortigosa Iron Bridge loop from El Rasillo de Cameros&lt;/a&gt;, which is about 6 km long. Another accessible option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14938917" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of San Martín – ErmitaDeSanPedro loop from Torrecilla en Cameros&lt;/a&gt;, covering approximately 5.7 km.

Q: Can I find trails with waterfalls in the Torrecilla En Cameros area?
A: Absolutely. The region is known for its water features. A popular trail that leads to a waterfall is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14938935" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cemetery – Viguera Waterfall (Chorrón de Viguera) loop from Viguera&lt;/a&gt;. You can also find stunning waterfalls within the Sierra de Cebollera Natural Park and the Chorrón de Viguera.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes available in Torrecilla En Cameros?
A: Yes, many routes in the area are circular. A highly rated option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e989456943/cueva-ventana-ermita-de-nuestra-senora-de-la-esperanza-iglesia-de-san-martin-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cueva Ventana, Ermita de Nuestra Señora de la Esperanza, Iglesia de San Martín – circular route&lt;/a&gt;, which is 6.6 km long and takes about 2 hours. Another is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31814631" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Viewpoint – San Pedro Apostol Church loop from Viguera&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What kind of natural landscapes can I expect to see while hiking here?
A: Hiking in Torrecilla En Cameros offers diverse landscapes, from dramatic mountainous terrain and picturesque river valleys carved by the Iregua River to dense beech and pine forests, especially within the Sierra de Cebollera Natural Park. You'll also encounter impressive canyons, such as the one carved by the River Leza, and varied flora including hazelnut trees, poplars, and oak forests.

Q: Are the hiking trails in Torrecilla En Cameros dog-friendly?
A: Many trails in the Torrecilla En Cameros region are suitable for dogs, allowing you to enjoy the natural beauty with your canine companion. It's always recommended to keep dogs on a leash, especially in natural parks or areas with wildlife, and to check local regulations for specific routes.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or attractions I can visit near the trails?
A: Beyond the trails, you can explore several interesting attractions. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5321537" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Olmo de El Rasillo&lt;/a&gt; is a notable natural monument. The Ortigosa Caves, a short drive away, are the only caves in La Rioja open to tourists. You can also visit the ancient &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5191607" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Anguiano&lt;/a&gt; settlement or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3108991" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Peñuejos Rock Formations&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: When is the best time of year to go hiking in Torrecilla En Cameros?
A: The region is beautiful year-round, but autumn is particularly vibrant when the beech forests turn brilliant reds and yellows. Spring offers lush greenery and pleasant temperatures. Summer is ideal for higher altitude trails, while winter can provide unique snowy landscapes, though some routes might be less accessible.

Q: Is there public transport access to trailheads in the region?
A: While Torrecilla En Cameros is well-connected by road, public transport options directly to all trailheads can be limited. It's advisable to check local bus schedules for connections to main villages like Torrecilla en Cameros or Viguera, and plan for car access or local taxis for reaching more remote starting points.

Q: Where can I find parking for hiking trails in Torrecilla En Cameros?
A: Parking is generally available in the villages that serve as starting points for many trails, such as Torrecilla en Cameros itself, Viguera, or El Rasillo de Cameros. For specific trailheads, look for designated parking areas or roadside spots, especially near popular attractions or recreational areas.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Torrecilla En Cameros?
A: The trails around Torrecilla En Cameros are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 30 reviews. Hikers often praise the varied scenery, from river valleys to mountain views, and the well-maintained paths that cater to different abilities.

Q: Are there any caves I can explore near the hiking routes?
A: Yes, the Ortigosa Caves are a significant attraction near Torrecilla En Cameros. They are the only caves in La Rioja open for tourist visits, featuring spectacular stalactites and stalagmites across more than 350 square meters. They offer a fascinating underground experience to complement your hiking adventures.

Q: Are there any trails that offer great viewpoints?
A: Many trails in the mountainous Sierra de Cameros region offer spectacular viewpoints. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31814631" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Viewpoint – San Pedro Apostol Church loop from Viguera&lt;/a&gt; is specifically designed to showcase panoramic vistas. Peña Seto also offers breathtaking views from its summit, making it a rewarding hike for those seeking scenic overlooks.

Q: Can I find trails that follow ancient paths or historical routes?
A: Yes, the region is rich in history. You can journey back in time by following the Roman Road of the Iregua River, a simple hike that traces an ancient path alongside the river. The area also features historical structures like the Ermita de San Pedro, often reached via picturesque routes through walnut groves and oak forests.
tips: Curiously, it is just 50 meters from Cueva Mesa and Cueva Silla. Nearby there is a sign that says Cueva Lóbrega.
Small hermitage in the upper part of the town, next to the viewpoint.

It began to be built in the year 1545 in a masonry factory. It is a church with a rectangular plan, with a nave with three sections with chapels between buttresses, a transept and an octagonal apse with three levels. It is covered by star-shaped ribbed vaults of different layouts, and has slightly pointed arches. The three-section sacristy is a Baroque construction from the 17th and 18th centuries. The two-bodied tower is from the end of the 16th century.
Inside the parish church, the main altarpiece stands out, built in walnut wood, dating from the beginning of the 18th century.
